steps.title: Apply Pythagorean theorem with early factoring
steps.content:
For a right triangle, use the Pythagorean theorem. Notice both legs are divisible by 4: $28 = 4(7)$ and $20 = 4(5)$.
$$c^2 = [4(7)]^2 + [4(5)]^2 = 16(49) + 16(25) = 16(49 + 25) = 16(74)$$

steps.title: Simplify the radical to find the hypotenuse
steps.content:
Take the square root of both sides:
$$c = \sqrt{16(74)} = \sqrt{16} \cdot \sqrt{74} = 4\sqrt{74}$$
That confirms B is correct.
